schema_source: grande_finale.tex \label{prop:moment-sandwich} (L692-706, defines Gamma_r and the moment sandwich) + \label{thm:moment-q} (L708-727, the finite moment criterion) + cap25_cap_v13_raw.tex \label{prob:capfp-gamma} (L8135-8145, the fixed-moment collision hierarchy) + \label{prop:capfp-kernel} (L8173-8192, the moment-kernel dictionary) + \label{rem:capfp-gamma-measured} (L8228-8240, prior calibration values reproduced here and extended). First data/derivation packet measuring Gamma_r raw-vs-twist-primitive-removed on 5 toy rows, r=2..8, exact Fraction/big-int arithmetic; proves two exact structural bricks (unconditional order floor; monotone certificate) that upgrade PR #366's route-cut moment orders from Gamma_r-trivial estimates to unconditional lower bounds.
deliverable_name: The moment constant Gamma_r on the removed prefix object: benign growth, an exact order floor, and why removal does not help
status: PROVED-LOCAL(Brick 1: unconditional order floor; Brick 2: monotone certificate) / MEASURED(Gamma_r raw-vs-removed, 5 rows, r=2..8, exact Fraction arithmetic) / AUDIT(removal-does-not-help finding; Poisson closed-form model) / OPEN(dense-bulk law persistence at the deployed w = prob:row-sharp-q itself). Does not evaluate Gamma_r at any deployed w (astronomically out of reach); does not prove or refute prob:row-sharp-q; does not move the frontier edge; does not alter any verdict or integer of PR #366 (upgrades its orders to proved floors, does not touch its ladder margins).

measured_curves.method: exact Fraction arithmetic throughout the histogram and Gamma_r sums; float only in final log2 reports. Rows 1-3 by exhaustive itertools.combinations enumeration (C(n,m) work); row 4 (mu64_F257_34) by subset-size DP over the prefix ring B^w (avoids enumerating C(64,34)~2^60 subsets). Independently reproduced bit-for-bit in this packaging session against laneN_outputs.json on every (row,w,r) checked -- see the companion verifier's gate ii output.

rows.label: mu24_F97_12
rows.note: this row's w=2 is the packet's headline dense-bulk exhibit: log2(Gamma_8)=0.0550 < 0.17

measured_curves.regime_law: log2(Gamma_r) < 0.17 whenever mean fiber lambda >~ 100 (dense bulk); grows as a Bell/Touchard-polynomial law (log-log slope of log2 Gamma_r vs r measured ~2.4) only at the Poisson boundary lambda=O(1); blows up as lambda^{1-r} (exponential in r) only in the over-saturated lambda<1 regime. None of these toy w values reach the deployed w; the dense-bulk law's persistence to deployed w is EXPERIMENTAL extrapolation, not measured (see extrapolation block).
measured_curves.full_grid_note: Complete Gamma_r (raw + twist-primitive) grid, r=2..8, for all 4 rows across every measured w (F17x_16_8 w=1..4, mu20_F41_10 w=1..4, mu24_F97_12 w=1..4, mu64_F257_34 w=1..2) -- 14 (row,w) pairs, independently recomputed bit-for-bit against laneN_outputs.json in this packaging session (0 mismatches across all cells_hit, prim_cells, R_raw, R_prim, and all 14 Gamma_r raw/prim values per pair). This is the companion verifier's ground truth for gate ii/iii/iv/v.

brick1_order_floor.statement: Because Gamma_r>=1 for all r (prop:moment-sandwich / raw prob:capfp-gamma, power-mean fact, distribution-free), the finite moment criterion log2 Gamma_r + w log2|B| <= r Delta (thm:moment-q) forces r >= r0 := ceil(w log2|B| / Delta) unconditionally, achieved iff Gamma_r=1 (perfect prefix flatness) at that order, and lowerable by no removal, cancellation, or twist-orbit argument (the Gamma_r>=1 fact is distribution-free).
brick1_order_floor.proof: Set log2 Gamma_r = 0 (its infimum) in thm:moment-q's inequality log2 Gamma_r + w log2|B| <= r Delta; solving for r gives r >= w log2|B| / Delta, and since r is an integer, r >= ceil(w log2|B| / Delta). No assumption on the distribution of the prefix fiber is used beyond Gamma_r>=1, which holds unconditionally (power means / prop:moment-sandwich).

exact_integer_recompute.method: p (the field size) taken as the exact integer (P_KB=2^31-2^24+1=2130706433, P_M31=2^31-1=2147483647); w taken as the exact deployed integer; Delta taken as the 4-decimal value printed in grande_finale.tex's adjacent-margin table (L183-186); r0=ceil(w*log2(p)/Delta) computed in float64 (log2 of a ~31-bit integer has ~15-16 significant digits of headroom, no precision loss at this scale).

cross_check_vs_PR366.reading: PR #366's kb_mca_conjq_route_margins_v1.json computed r=ceil(w log2(p)/Delta) at L_0 and L_4 as heuristic route-cut estimates ('optimistic case log2(Gamma_r)~0'); this packet independently reproduces both integers exactly from raw constants AND proves the r>=r0 direction is unconditional (Gamma_r>=1 always, not merely 'optimistic'/assumed) -- upgrading #366's orders from assumed-trivial estimates to proved floors.

laneN_rounded_variant.method: laneN_compute.py's hardcoded 'deployed' table rounds log2|B| to 2 decimals (30.99 for KoalaBear, 31.00 for Mersenne-31) instead of using the exact integer field size; this is the source of the small discrepancy documented in found_vs_claimed_corrections below.

brick2_monotonicity.statement: r |-> R_eff(r) := Gamma_r^{1/(r-1)} is non-decreasing and increases to R = |B|^w max_z mu(z) as r->infinity (ell^p-norm monotonicity). Combined with the proved upper bound Gamma_r <= R^{r-1} (raw prop:capfp-anchors, L8160), this pins the exact minimal order at which the moment criterion (MC) CAN hold given the true R:
brick2_monotonicity.r_min_formula: r_min(R) = ceil( (w log2|B| - log2 R) / (Delta - log2 R) ), finite iff R < 2^Delta, -> r0 (Brick 1) as R->1, -> infinity as R up-arrow 2^Delta
brick2_monotonicity.monotonicity_verification: checked non-decreasing across r=2..8 on all 14 measured (row,w) pairs in laneN_outputs.json (100% pass); re-verified live on the subset recomputed by the companion verifier (gate iii)
brick2_monotonicity.reading: the moment method certifies R<=2^Delta at order r_min(R) IFF Q is true (R<2^Delta); it is a high-moment certificate FOR Q, not a route around it.

r_insensitivity_table.headline: doubling R (log2R: 0 -> 1) inflates KB-MCA's order from 94196 to 98639: a 4.718% increase. r_min is near-insensitive to R across 3 orders of magnitude of R-1; the moment order is robustly ~w log2|B| / Delta regardless of the (measured, benign, ~1) value of Gamma_r/R in the dense bulk.

removal_ledger.definition: the twist group H (order n) acts on prefix space (z_j)->(zeta^j z_j); a prefix value z is PRIMITIVE iff its twist stabilizer s(z):=gcd(n,{j:z_j!=0}) equals 1, quotient/periodic iff s(z)>1 (z=0 has s=n, the fully-periodic/planted fiber). Gamma_r splits additively into primitive-part + periodic-part; the primitive part is the 'removed' (quotient/planted-pruned) object.

dense_bulk_removal_effect.reading: in the dense bulk (the frontier's regime), quotient/planted removal shaves <0.08 bit off R and <0.04 bit off Gamma_8 -- negligible; the constants are already ~1 and need no taming.

poisson_boundary_removal_effect.reading: removal bites hard (up to 1.6 bits) only at the Poisson/over-saturated boundary, far from the frontier's dense-bulk regime; even there it is inconsistent (delta_R=0 at some dense rows where the heaviest fiber is itself already primitive, e.g. mu20_F41_10 w=2,3).

exact_gamma2_kernel_ledger.identity: sum_z N_w(z)^2 = C(n,m) + sum_{ell>=delta} C(n-2 ell, m-ell) T_w(ell)  (raw prop:capfp-kernel(c), L8173-8192)
exact_gamma2_kernel_ledger.reading: the quotient (removable) off-diagonal is a small fraction of Gamma_2 (0.015%-5.6% across w=1..4 on this row, per laneN Sec 2.D); the primitive/aperiodic part carries the mass. Independently reproduced bit-for-bit by this packaging session's own reimplementation (see companion verifier gate iv).

poisson_model.form: Gamma_r ~ E_{N~Poisson(lambda)}[(N/lambda)^r] = B_r(lambda)/lambda^r, B_r the Bell/Touchard polynomial, lambda=C(n,m)|B|^-w the mean fiber
poisson_model.status: heuristic brick, labeled; captures the regime and growth EXPONENT (matched by data) but not the exact constant (real tail measured 10-40% heavier than Poisson at the boundary; the full-multiplicative-group rows are sub-Poisson/flatter by twist-orbit symmetry, measured Gamma_8 0.09x the Poisson value at one row)
predictions_matched_by_data: lambda>>1 (dense/frontier): Gamma_r -> 1 (matches Gamma_r=1.000000 to machine precision at the ultra-dense toy row)
lambda=O(1) (Poisson boundary): log2 Gamma_r ~ r log r (Bell growth) -- matches measured log-log slope ~2.4
lambda<1 (over-saturated): Gamma_r ~ lambda^{1-r} -- matches measured linear-in-r log2 slopes of 3-5.6

frontier_lambda_crosscheck.definition: log2(lambda) = log2 C(n,m) - w log2(p), independently recomputed here via math.lgamma (no huge exact integers needed)
frontier_lambda_crosscheck.reading: laneN_analysis.md asserts 'the frontier's lambda=2^{35.7}' in prose without an accompanying JSON computation (out of toy-row reach); this packet independently recomputes it from raw N=2^21, K=2^20, M_SAFE=1116048, W_SAFE=67471 (the same constants verify_conjq_rung_routes_dead.py uses) and finds log2(lambda)=35.7352, matching Lane N's claim to the printed digit. It ALSO matches, independently, Lane M's concurrent in-flight cross-check (margins_table[0].log2_floor=35.7352 in the uncommitted saturated_bc_budget_fit_v1.json, derived from the DIFFERENT margin_identity route margin=log2(B*)-log2(C(n,a+)*p^-w)) -- a three-way cross-validation (Lane N prose, Lane M's independent JSON, this packet's from-scratch recompute) across two concurrent, mutually-uninformed lanes. All five deployed rows land at log2(lambda) in [20.7,36] -- solidly >100 in linear terms (2^20.7 ~ 1.7e6), i.e. deep in the measured 'dense bulk' regime where the toy data shows Gamma_r=1+o(1).

found_vs_claimed_corrections.found: the underlying raw value is 680396.0299381712 (laneN_outputs.json _extrapolation, M31-list row, 'R=1(flat,#366)'); ceil(680396.0299...) = 680397, not 680396. This is a rounding/truncation slip in laneN_analysis.md's prose (off by 1 in ~680397, ~0.00015% relative), not a computation error in laneN_outputs.json itself (whose stored float is correct).
found_vs_claimed_corrections.action: this packet's note and JSON use 680397 for M31-list throughout; not silently fixed in laneN_analysis.md (a Lane N working file, not part of this PR) -- flagged here instead.

found_vs_claimed_corrections.item: Brick 1 floors for KB-MCA, KB-list, L4-rung: two valid figures differing by rounding precision, not by error
found_vs_claimed_corrections.claimed_in: laneN_analysis.md Sec 3 / Sec 5 and laneN_outputs.json's _extrapolation table: r0 = 94200 (KB-MCA), 94996 (KB-list), 5889 (L4), computed via w*log2B/Delta with log2B hardcoded to 2 decimals (30.99 KoalaBear, 31.00 Mersenne-31) in laneN_compute.py's 'deployed' list
found_vs_claimed_corrections.found: recomputing with the EXACT integer field size (P_KB=2^31-2^24+1, log2=30.98868468744926) instead of the rounded 30.99 gives 94196 (KB-MCA), 94992 (KB-list), 5886 (L4) -- differences of -4, -4, -3 respectively, fully explained by the 2-decimal log2(p) rounding (a ~0.0013% relative difference in log2(p), amplified by w~67471 or w~4216). Mersenne-31 rows are essentially unaffected (log2(p_M31)=30.999999999328193 rounds to 31.00 with negligible error), matching exactly either way (641584, 680397).
found_vs_claimed_corrections.action: this packet ships the exact-integer figures (94196/94992/641584/680397/5886) as primary, since they (a) use genuinely exact arithmetic from raw constants and (b) independently reproduce PR #366's own shipped values (94196 at L_0, 5886 at L_4) to the digit -- a stronger form of the 'upgrades #366' claim. laneN's rounded-log2B variant is recorded alongside (brick1_order_floor.laneN_rounded_variant) as a documented, explained near-miss, not a contradiction.

non_claims: does not evaluate Gamma_r at any deployed w (67471, 67447, or any full-scale row) -- astronomically out of computational reach; all measured Gamma_r data is on toy rows w<=4

the dense-bulk law 'lambda>>1 implies Gamma_r=1+o(1)' is measured only at w<=4 (ultra-dense only to w=2); its persistence to deployed w (67471/67447) is UNMEASURED EXTRAPOLATION and is exactly prob:row-sharp-q itself -- labeled EXPERIMENTAL throughout
does not prove or refute prob:row-sharp-q, prob:saturated-bc, or any promoted theorem/problem
does not move the frontier edge (1116047,1116048) or any adjacent agreement
does not alter any verdict, integer, or margin of PR #366 (upgrades its route-cut orders to proved unconditional floors; does not touch its ladder shapes, H1/H2 margins, or verdicts)
the Poisson closed-form model (Sec 6 of the companion note) is a heuristic curve-fit, not a theorem; its constant is measurably off (10-40%) at the Poisson boundary
Brick 1 and Brick 2 are one-line consequences of already-proved facts (Gamma_r>=1 power means; Gamma_r<=R^{r-1} anchors) -- they carry no new proof risk, but also settle nothing new about R itself
the frontier mean-fiber lambda values (frontier_lambda_crosscheck) are independent recomputations for cross-validation, not new measurements of Gamma_r at those rows
